title: Enforcing TOTP for All Users
description:
## Make Everyone Set Up and Use TOTP

Security requires that **all users** in the realm set up an authenticator app (TOTP) and use it at every login, starting **at their next login**. How do you enforce this in Keycloak?

options.text: Set the **OTP execution to REQUIRED** in the browser flow, and add the **Configure OTP** required action (as a default) so users without OTP are forced to enroll.
Do nothing — Keycloak prompts every user for OTP automatically by default.
Enable a checkbox in the **client** settings; OTP is a per-client toggle only.
Email each user a static code and ask them to keep entering it.

explanation: Keycloak supports **TOTP/HOTP** via authenticator apps, governed by the realm's **OTP Policy**. To *enforce* it you make the **OTP execution REQUIRED** (or CONDITIONAL) in the browser authentication flow, and you add the **Configure OTP** required action — set as a default required action so every user who hasn't enrolled is forced to set up TOTP at next login. Option **b** is false: OTP is **not** on by default; you must enable it. Option **c** is wrong — MFA enforcement is driven by authentication flows and required actions at the realm level, not a simple per-client checkbox. Option **d** describes a static shared code, which is not OTP and defeats the purpose of a per-login one-time password.

hints.title: Two levers
hints.body: Required action = force enrollment; OTP execution requirement in the flow = force its use at login. You usually need both.
